summary: Matched a small but dependency-sensitive piece of sysdolphin baselib JObj code by moving `HSD_JObjCheckDepend` into C, removing the old assembly body, adding the new C object to linker order, and tightening JObj header declarations/inline flag checks. The PR body notes that JObj work could not be pushed until the full file was done because inlines affected codegen, making this a useful example of handling inline-heavy baselib matching carefully.
what_changed: Added `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c` containing a C implementation of `HSD_JObjCheckDepend(HSD_JObj* jobj)`.
Removed the assembly implementation previously labeled `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` from `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s`.

Updated remaining assembly call sites in `HSD_JObjAnim`, `func_803707F8`, and `HSD_JObjAnimAll` from `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` to `HSD_JObjCheckDepend` while preserving the same branch targets/offsets in the excerpt.
Changed the public declaration in `include/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h` from `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` to `HSD_JObjCheckDepend`.
Annotated `HSD_JObj` struct members with explicit offsets, including `flags` at `0x14`, `aobj` at `0x7C`, `robj` at `0x80`, and `id` at `0x84`.
Replaced magic flag constants in JObj inlines with symbolic names: `USER_DEF_MTX`, `MTX_DIRTY`, and `MTX_INDEP_SRT`.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s.o` entry.
Adjusted `.sdata` handling in `jobj.s` by replacing concrete `lbl_804D5DA0`/`lbl_804D5DA8` string definitions with `.set` aliases based on `jobj_c_sdata_start` near `lbl_804D5DB0`.
smart_moves: Kept the asm remainder and the newly matched C object linked together, rather than attempting to convert all of `jobj.s` in one step.
Renamed the dependency check consistently across header and asm references, avoiding a stale `CheckDependAll` symbol after moving the function to C.

Used named flag macros in inline helpers and the new C function, making bit tests like matrix dirty/user-defined matrix/independent SRT easier to audit than raw constants such as `0x40`, `0x800000`, and `0x2000000`.
Added struct offset comments before matching more JObj code, which helps validate field layout against assembly loads/stores such as `flags` at `0x14`, `parent` at `0x0C`, and `robj` at `0x80`.
Handled small-data labels explicitly with `.set` aliases, a useful tactic when newly compiled C contributes strings/sdata but the surrounding assembly still references old labels.
decomp_lessons: Inline-heavy files can require matching or staging a larger surrounding unit before a small function can be safely landed; the PR body explicitly calls out that JObj could not be pushed earlier due to inline usage.
When converting one function out of a large assembly file, update all remaining assembly call labels to the new C symbol name so mixed C/asm linkage remains coherent.
Prefer symbolic flag constants in headers and inlines once the bit meaning is known; it documents semantics and reduces mistakes in future matching.
Offset-annotated structs are especially valuable for baselib object types where many assembly functions access fields by fixed offsets.
Small-data/string label placement can be a matching blocker; aliases or label arithmetic may be needed to preserve addresses while migrating code to C.
naming_conventions: `HSD_JObjCheckDepend` replaced the older/stale `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` name in both declaration and call sites.
JObj helper names follow the `HSD_JObj...` prefix, e.g. `HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty`, `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslate`, and `HSD_JObjResetRST`.
Flag names are uppercase semantic macros such as `USER_DEF_MTX`, `MTX_DIRTY`, `MTX_INDEP_SRT`, `MTX_INDEP_PARENT`, `EFFECTOR`, `JOINT1`, and `JOINT2`.
Struct definitions use explicit offset comments in the form `/* 0x14 */ u32 flags;` to tie C layout back to assembly evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed exactly the matched assembly body from `jobj.s` and left later assembly functions in place.
Preserved remaining assembly branch/call disassembly while changing symbol names from `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` to `HSD_JObjCheckDepend`.
Inserted `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.o` before `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s.o` in linker order so the new C function occupies the expected location before the assembly tail.
Used `.set jobj_c_sdata_start, lbl_804D5DB0 - 0x10`, then `.set lbl_804D5DA0` and `.set lbl_804D5DA8` to keep legacy sdata labels available after moving data generation toward C.
The matched C implementation relies on `HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty` inline behavior and flag combinations to reproduce the old dependency-dirtying logic.

review_feedback.body: Up-to-date with `master` and `OK`. Should merge imo.

follow_up_queries: Check later JObj PRs for whether the remaining `asm/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s` functions were migrated using the same inline and sdata tactics.
Investigate whether the full-file-looking changes in `include/functions.h` and `obj_files.mk` were formatting/line-ending churn or generated ordering changes beyond adding `jobj.c.o`.
Search for future cleanup of `HSD_JObjCheckDependAll` references to confirm the renamed symbol fully replaced the old name.
